page: OVHcloud l4-360 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 90 dedicated physical cores powered by the AMD EPYC 9454 processor, operating at a base frequency of 2.75 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 360.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB per core. For hardware acceleration, the server integrates four NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined total of 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core and cryptographic capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Passmark CPU encryption test both place the instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 707.28 GB/sec, also ranking in the top tier. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware configuration achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference, processing 2772.24 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 54.3 tokens/sec for text generation using a medium 7B model. Single-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2070.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 3138.77, placing it in the top 25% and top 10% respectively.

From a resource density perspective, the l4-360 offers high compute and accelerator density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that can leverage multiple GPUs and dedicated CPU cores simultaneously. However, the local storage capacity of 400 GB NVMe SSD is relatively modest compared to the massive compute and memory footprint, which may require external storage solutions for very large datasets. This server is suited for large-scale LLM inference, machine learning model training, parallel data processing, software compilation, and cryptography-heavy workloads.
description: OVHcloud l4-360 is a GPU-accelerated server instance featuring 90 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores running at 2.75 GHz, paired with 360.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 96 GB of VRAM, alongside a 400 GB NVMe SSD and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core processing, cryptography, and LLM inference, with the system processing 2772.24 tokens/sec for 7B model prompt processing. While the 400 GB local storage is modest relative to the compute capacity, the high density of dedicated cores and GPUs makes this instance highly efficient for parallelized tasks. The server is suited for machine learning inference, data science, software compilation, and cryptographic workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud l4-360 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 90 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 360 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, and 25 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for LLM inference and parallel computing.
meta_description: OVHcloud l4-360 is a KVM-based server with 90 AMD EPYC cores, 360 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA L4 GPUs, designed for high-throughput parallel workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-GPU server combining ninety physical AMD EPYC cores with four NVIDIA L4 accelerators for intensive parallel computing and machine learning.
bullet_points: Ninety dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores operating at 2.75 GHz.
Four NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs providing 96 GB of total VRAM.
360 GB of system memory and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD.
Top-tier LLM inference and multi-threaded decompression performance.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address.
Suited for machine learning, software compilation, and cryptographic tasks.
